title: The One Where Joey Speaks French

jokes.text: Oh, he's at the doctor's, he didn't poop the whole time we were there.
jokes.setup: Ross asks where Mike is after their romantic honeymoon
jokes.punchline: The overshare about Mike's digestive issues
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe overshare that contrasts romantic expectations with bodily function reality. Well-constructed misdirection.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So where's Mike? I thought you two were going to spend the whole day together after your honeymoon.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, he's in the bathroom. He ate some bad sushi last night and now he's got like the worst digestive thing happening. It's like, explosions. Actual explosions. I can hear them from out here. It's like a war zone in there.

jokes.text: which according to my resume, I'm fluent in.
jokes.setup: Joey mentions needing to speak French for an audition
jokes.punchline: Reveals he lied on his resume about being fluent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character beat - his casual dishonesty about skills he doesn't have is very in-character.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I need to speak French for this audition.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, you don't speak French.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know, but I put it on my resume.

jokes.text: BTW, how was that year-long dig in Cairo?
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel lecture Joey about not lying on resumes
jokes.punchline: Immediate revelation that Ross also lies on his resume
jokes.explanation: Excellent hypocrisy reveal. The immediate contradiction after moral lecturing creates perfect ironic timing.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Joey, you can't lie on your resume. It's dishonest and it always comes back to bite you.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's really not worth it. Just be honest about your qualifications.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, I get it.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm serious. I've never lied on mine, and look how well things have turned out for me.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, what about that time you said you were fluent in French?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That... that was different.

jokes.text: It was ok.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks about Ross's fictional Cairo dig
jokes.punchline: Ross's understated response to his imaginary adventure
jokes.explanation: Solid deadpan follow-up to the resume lie reveal, though not particularly memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So tell me about your dig in Cairo.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, it was... it was really something. Very sandy.

jokes.text: Oh, you're so sexy. / Seriously, stop or I'm gonna jump on you.
jokes.setup: Phoebe agrees to help Joey learn French
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly sexual response to his terrible French attempts
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's bizarre attraction to Joey's nonsense establishes the central joke premise well.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, so you want to learn French? I can help you with that.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Great! So how do you say 'hello' in French?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: It's 'bonjour.' Now you try.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Bon... jour?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that was... that was really good. Like, really good.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah? Okay, how about 'goodbye'?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That's 'au revoir.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Ooh... ruh-vwah?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh wow. That's... I need a minute. Is it hot in here?

jokes.text: Oh hey, when she gets here, is it ok if I introduce you two as my wife and the woman who's carrying my child?
jokes.setup: Chandler mentions introducing Monica and Erica
jokes.punchline: The awkward phrasing that makes Monica sound like a surrogate
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that highlights the unusual adoption situation through awkward phrasing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So I was thinking, we should introduce Monica and Erica.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, great idea. What are you gonna say, 'Hey Monica, this is Erica, the woman who's carrying your baby'?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, when you say it like that, it sounds weird.

jokes.text: No? Divorce?
jokes.setup: Monica rejects his introduction phrasing
jokes.punchline: Chandler's immediate leap to divorce as the solution
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - two words that perfectly capture Chandler's dramatic overreaction tendency.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, you can't introduce me like that.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Because it makes me sound like a piece of furniture!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, then we'll just get a divorce.

jokes.text: I want the baby to come out all cute and fat.
jokes.setup: Monica is making cookies for Erica
jokes.punchline: Her belief that cookies will make the baby fat and cute
jokes.explanation: Monica's controlling nature applied to an unborn baby she can't actually control is very in-character.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm making these cookies for Erica. I want the baby to be fat and cute.

jokes.text: and, you know, there's always some idiot who goes 'Moo'.
jokes.setup: Chandler complains about crowds at tourist spots being like cattle
jokes.punchline: His complaint about people mooing
jokes.explanation: Sets up the callback nicely, establishing Chandler's annoyance with this behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know what? I hate tourist spots. Everyone's packed in like cattle.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And the worst part? They actually start mooing!

jokes.text: Well, if it annoys you so much, then why do you do it?
jokes.setup: Chandler's complaint about people mooing at tourist spots
jokes.punchline: Monica revealing that Chandler is the idiot who moos
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback payoff that perfectly captures Chandler's self-defeating behavior patterns.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why do people always moo at cows when they go to farms or petting zoos? It's so stupid.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, you're the one who moos at cows.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's different!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No it's not.

jokes.text: My father had a heart attack... while I was at Barney's.
jokes.setup: Ross sarcastically asks if Rachel was shopping at Barney's
jokes.punchline: She actually was at Barney's during her father's heart attack
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - the timing makes Ross's sarcasm accidentally accurate, creating perfect awkward moment.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You were shopping at Barney's?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I was at Barney's when my father had his heart attack.

jokes.text: Still, still, let me come... for me.
jokes.setup: Rachel insists she doesn't need Ross to come to the hospital
jokes.punchline: Ross admitting he needs to come for his own sake
jokes.explanation: Shows Ross's neediness masquerading as concern, very true to his character.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I don't need you to come to the hospital, Ross. I'll be fine.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, I have to come. Not for you—for me. I need to be there when they tell you you're okay, so I can say 'I told you so.'

jokes.text: Really? It sounds exactly the same to me.
jokes.setup: Phoebe says Joey's French attempt isn't quite right
jokes.punchline: Joey thinks his gibberish sounds exactly like Phoebe's French
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey obliviousness - his complete lack of language awareness is very in-character.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That's not French, Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yes it is! It sounds exactly like your French!

jokes.text: Monica, calm, self.
jokes.setup: Monica is excited about meeting Erica
jokes.punchline: Her talking to herself to stay calm
jokes.explanation: Monica's need to verbally coach herself shows her anxiety and control issues perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, okay. You can do this. Just be yourself. She's going to love you. Well, not love you love you, but like you. No, that sounds weird. Just... be normal. Normal Monica. Not crazy Monica. Oh God, I'm already being crazy Monica, aren't I?

jokes.text: Well, welcome to NYC, or should I say ...
jokes.setup: Phoebe starts to say something clever about NYC
jokes.punchline: She has nothing clever to say
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's failed attempt at being clever creates a nice awkward beat.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I was gonna say something really clever about New York, but... I got nothing.

jokes.text: This baby better be really good.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler are doing tourist activities for Erica
jokes.punchline: Chandler's transactional view of the adoption process
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant - Chandler treating the adoption like a business transaction is both horrible and hilarious.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We're doing all these things for Erica. We're taking her to the Statue of Liberty, we're taking her to the top of the Empire State Building... I feel like we're supposed to give her a brochure and ask her to decide.

jokes.text: Ross, please. This is a hospital, ok? That actually means sth here.
jokes.setup: Ross introduces himself as Dr. Ross Geller
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dismissive comment about his PhD being meaningless in a medical context
jokes.explanation: Great dig at Ross's academic pretensions in a medical setting where his doctorate is irrelevant.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: A PhD? That's great, but it's not like you're a real doctor or anything.

jokes.text: Ross, please, don't be so scared of him. / I am not scared of him, I'm really sick. / I'm feeling better.
jokes.setup: Ross claims he's too sick to see Rachel's father
jokes.punchline: He immediately feels better when told the father is unconscious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross cowardice reveal - his 'illness' magically cures when the threat is removed.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You can't be serious. My dad is in the hospital and you're not coming?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know, I know, but I'm really sick. I can barely get out of bed.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, he's unconscious anyway, so he won't even know you're there.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh, well in that case... I feel much better already!

jokes.text: Oh, wow, that ear and nose hair trimmer I got him was just money down the drain, huh?
jokes.setup: Rachel sees her unconscious father
jokes.punchline: Her focus on his nose/ear hair rather than his condition
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that deflects from the serious situation with a superficial observation.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Dad! Are you okay? Dad?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Does anyone have tweezers? His ear hair is out of control.

jokes.text: No, when you put your feet up on my bed, you tucked on my cathode.
jokes.setup: Ross asks if the TV woke Dr. Green
jokes.punchline: Dr. Green's malapropism mixing 'catheter' with 'cathode'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that makes medical equipment sound like TV technology while explaining why he woke up.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Did the TV wake you up?

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Green
dialogue.line: No, no, I was already up. I couldn't sleep so I was in the living room watching the news. They were talking about some new medical procedure... something about a catheter... or was it a cathode? I don't know, these medical terms all sound the same to me.

jokes.text: What? A heart attack is sitting here talking to you.
jokes.setup: Ross asks if the heart attack is painful
jokes.punchline: Dr. Green implies talking to Ross is more painful than a heart attack
jokes.explanation: Brutal insult delivered with perfect timing - suggests Ross is more painful than cardiac arrest.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Is it painful?

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Green
dialogue.line: A little. Not as painful as this conversation.

jokes.text: So what's new with you, huh? Knocked up any more of my daughters lately?
jokes.setup: Small talk between Dr. Green and Ross
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Ross impregnating Rachel
jokes.explanation: Dr. Green's hostile small talk treats pregnancy like a casual hobby, creating uncomfortable humor.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Green
dialogue.line: So, how are things going with you and Rachel?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, uh, things are good. Really good, actually.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Green
dialogue.line: That's wonderful. I assume you're being careful?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? Dad, we're fine.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Green
dialogue.line: Because the last thing we need is for you to get her pregnant.

jokes.text: Nope! Just the one. Rach!
jokes.setup: Dr. Green asks if Ross has impregnated more daughters
jokes.punchline: Ross's panicked response and desperate call for Rachel
jokes.explanation: Ross's terrified response to Dr. Green's hostility shows his cowardice perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? No! I mean, I don't think so. Rachel? RACHEL?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: How many daughters do you have?

jokes.text: What are you doing? I have to go before pull your head through a wall!
jokes.setup: Joey continues to butcher French pronunciation
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's threat of violence due to frustration
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's violent frustration contrasts with her usually peaceful nature, creating unexpected humor.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: How do you say 'croissant'?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: It's 'cwa-sahn.' Joey, if you say it wrong one more time, I'm going to kill you.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, okay... 'craw-sant.'

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That's it!

jokes.text: I think we solve the mystery of heart attack.
jokes.setup: Ross finds only bacon and heavy cream in Dr. Green's fridge
jokes.punchline: Connecting the unhealthy diet to the heart attack
jokes.explanation: Straightforward observational humor connecting diet to medical emergency.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, there's your problem right here. Look at this fridge — bacon and heavy cream. That's it. No wonder he had a heart attack.

jokes.text: Just can't believe I'm in Rachel Green's room. / Like I have ever been in Rachel Green's room.
jokes.setup: Ross acts amazed to be in Rachel's childhood room
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic correction revealing he's been there many times
jokes.explanation: Ross's sarcasm about his teenage years with Rachel, though not particularly strong.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wow, Rachel's childhood room. I can't believe I've never been in here before.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? Yes, you have.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh right, yeah. Many times.

jokes.text: But we do.
jokes.setup: Rachel says you don't expect doctors to get sick
jokes.punchline: Ross's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Simple correction that's mildly amusing but not particularly crafted or memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You don't expect doctors to get sick.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Actually, doctors get sick all the time. We're constantly exposed to germs and pathogens.

jokes.text: Rachel Green is very happy you're in her room.
jokes.setup: Earlier joke about Ross being in 'Rachel Green's room'
jokes.punchline: Ross continuing the third-person joke
jokes.explanation: Mild callback to the earlier full-name joke, maintaining the bit but not adding much.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Ross is very excited about this development.

jokes.text: What taking advantage? I'm giving you the advantage, enjoy!
jokes.setup: Ross says he doesn't want to take advantage of Rachel
jokes.punchline: Rachel's literal interpretation and reversal of 'advantage'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that turns Ross's noble gesture into a business transaction.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I don't want to take advantage of you.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, maybe I want to take advantage of you.

jokes.text: Haven't had sex in 4 months, I should get a medal for that!
jokes.setup: Ross leaves Rachel's room after rejecting her advances
jokes.punchline: His private complaint about sexual frustration
jokes.explanation: Ross's sexual frustration undermining his noble gesture creates relatable self-pity humor.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I mean, come on! She's right there, she wants me, and I'm supposed to just... what? Pretend I don't notice? This is torture. Actual torture. I have a PhD and I'm being intellectually outsmarted by my own biology here.

jokes.text: Yeah, you missed a lot when you were mooing.
jokes.setup: Chandler appreciates NYC tourist spots differently
jokes.punchline: Monica's reference to his mooing habit
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that connects Chandler's behavior to missing out on actual experiences.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, you can't just stand in the middle of Times Square and moo at people.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why not? Nobody else is doing it.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's exactly why you shouldn't be doing it!

jokes.text: How is that now? / Well, there is a chance it's another guy.
jokes.setup: Erica says the father 'almost definitely' is the football player
jokes.punchline: Revelation there's uncertainty about paternity
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the bigger reveal about the second potential father.

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: The father is almost definitely the football player.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Almost definitely? What does that mean?

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: Well, there were a few guys...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A few guys?! How many is a few?

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: I don't know, like... five or six.

jokes.text: I mean, I've only ever been with 2 guys but they sorta overlapped.
jokes.setup: Discussion about who the father might be
jokes.punchline: Erica's casual admission of overlapping relationships
jokes.explanation: Erica's innocent delivery of complicated relationship timeline creates cringe humor.

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: Well, there's Frank, and there's Gary, and there's... you know what, there's also Dave from my aerobics class.

jokes.text: No, he's in prison. / No. He killed his father with a shovel.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler hope the other guy went to college too
jokes.punchline: The revelation about murder with increasingly specific details
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from 'in prison' to specific patricide details. Dark but brilliantly structured.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So did you go to college?

dialogue.speaker: Erica's boyfriend
dialogue.line: No, I didn't.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh.

dialogue.speaker: Erica's boyfriend
dialogue.line: I killed a man.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Erica's boyfriend
dialogue.line: With a rifle.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay...

dialogue.speaker: Erica's boyfriend
dialogue.line: From 600 yards away.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's... that's not what we meant by college.

jokes.text: But other than that he's a great guy. / I'll bet his dad doesn't think so.
jokes.setup: Erica defends the murderer's character
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deadpan observation about the victim's opinion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan response that highlights the absurdity of defending a patricidal boyfriend.

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: He's not a murderer, he's a good man. He just made one mistake.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, the victim might disagree with you on that.

jokes.text: Honey, it's us. Of course it's the shovel killer.
jokes.setup: Monica hopes it could be the football player
jokes.punchline: Chandler's fatalistic acceptance of their bad luck
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler fatalism - accepting that of course they'd get the murderer's baby. Great character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, no, no. It's not going to be the football player. It's going to be some guy named Ugly Steve or something.

jokes.text: I'll keep an eye on him. / Easy for you to say. He's a father killer. / He probably loves his mommy.
jokes.setup: Monica suggests keeping an eye on the baby
jokes.punchline: Chandler's logic that the baby will probably love his mother
jokes.explanation: Dark logic about inherited murderous tendencies, suggesting Monica should be safe.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We should probably keep an eye on the baby.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why? The baby will probably love his mother.

jokes.text: He's probably got a tattoo says 'Mom' on the shovel wielding arm.
jokes.setup: Chandler's theory about the murderer loving his mother
jokes.punchline: Specific image of 'Mom' tattoo on the murder weapon arm
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual of combining traditional 'Mom' tattoo with patricide weapon. Dark but perfectly crafted.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The murderer loves his mother. That's why he has a 'Mom' tattoo on the arm holding the murder weapon.

jokes.text: See?
jokes.setup: Joey demonstrates his improved French to Phoebe
jokes.punchline: His proud delivery of complete gibberish
jokes.explanation: Joey's confidence in his nonsense French is perfectly in character and absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Bonjour, Phoebe. Comment allez-vous? Je suis très heureux de vous voir.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wow, Joey, that's amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Merci. Et maintenant, quelque chose de plus difficile. Le fromage est dans les pantalons du elephant qui danse avec les nuages invisibles.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: ...What does that mean?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I have no idea. But I'm pretty sure I nailed it.

jokes.text: Again, you're not SPEAKING FRENCH!
jokes.setup: Joey's continued gibberish
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's exasperated emphasis on the obvious
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's frustrated statement of the obvious creates good escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Joey, that's not even a word!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It could be!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, it really couldn't!

jokes.text: For one thing, the guy on the tape said I was doing a good job!
jokes.setup: Joey thinks he'll get the French-speaking part
jokes.punchline: His evidence is encouragement from a language tape
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - taking encouragement from a recorded language lesson as personal validation.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm totally gonna get this part. The guy on the French tape told me I was doing great.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, that's a tape. It says that to everyone.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, he was talking directly to me. I could feel it.

jokes.text: In the future, when a girl asks for some ill-advised sympathy sex, just do it.
jokes.setup: Rachel is angry at Ross for rejecting her advances
jokes.punchline: Her practical advice about sympathy sex
jokes.explanation: Rachel's blunt relationship advice with good economy and quotable phrasing.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You know what, Ross? If you're not interested, fine. But let me give you some practical advice: sympathy sex is a lot easier to explain than sympathy murder.

jokes.text: I would have woken up feeling comforted and satisfied.
jokes.setup: Rachel explains how Ross's rejection made her feel
jokes.punchline: Her clinical description of desired post-sex feelings
jokes.explanation: Rachel's matter-of-fact approach to casual sex creates mild cringe humor.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I wanted to feel like I just had the best sex of my life. I wanted to feel... satisfied, you know? Like, completely and totally satisfied.

jokes.text: I gotta say I have not had sex a lot times before, this is the worst ever.
jokes.setup: Argument about their non-sex encounter
jokes.punchline: Rachel rating their non-encounter as the worst sex ever
jokes.explanation: Brilliant paradox - rating sex that didn't happen as the worst sex ever. Great escalation of absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That was the worst sex I've ever had.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We didn't have sex.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: What? That's right, sex is off the table! I'm never having sex with you again.
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel are arguing about not having sex
jokes.punchline: Rachel banning sex they weren't having anyway
jokes.explanation: Ironic declaration that makes their already non-existent sex life officially non-existent.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Fine! I'm banning sex!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You can't ban something we weren't having anyway.

jokes.text: If the baby is a boy, would you name him after my father Jiminy Billy Bob? / Noo. You were right, that was fun.
jokes.setup: Erica makes what sounds like a serious request
jokes.punchline: Revealed to be a joke, with Chandler's relief
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that makes audience think Erica is adding another complication before revealing it's a joke.

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: I need to talk to you about something serious.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh God, what?

dialogue.speaker: Erica
dialogue.line: I'm kidding! You should see your face!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh thank God.

jokes.text: Oh, God, it's Shovelly Joe, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Monica asks if Chandler found out who the father is
jokes.punchline: Monica's nickname for the murderer
jokes.explanation: Monica's cute nickname for a patricidal murderer creates good callback humor.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Did Chandler find out who the father is?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, it's the guy from the coffee shop! We call him 'The Stabber.'

jokes.text: Because the thing she did with the prison guy would be pretty hard to make a baby that way.
jokes.setup: Chandler explains how he knows the murderer isn't the father
jokes.punchline: Implicit reference to non-reproductive sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Adult humor that implies specific sexual details without being explicit.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, the murderer can't be the father because... you know... he's been... busy.

jokes.text: Oh, God. What was it? The thing we hardly ever do or the thing we never do? / The thing we never do.
jokes.setup: Monica wants to know what sexual act Erica did
jokes.punchline: The classification system for their sex life
jokes.explanation: Brilliant categorization of sexual activities in a married couple's repertoire. Highly quotable and clever.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So what did you guys do?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, we started with A, then we moved on to B, and we ended up at C.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: A, B, C? What does that mean?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Above the waist, below the waist, and... car.

jokes.text: Uh, I am Ugeon Phillogne.
jokes.setup: Joey fails his French audition
jokes.punchline: His attempt to save face with a fake French identity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey desperation move - creating a fake French persona when caught in his lie.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Bonjour, je m'appelle... uh... Pierre. I am French actor. Very famous in France.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, you just bombed the audition five minutes ago.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That wasn't me. That was my American cousin, also named Joey. Very common name in France.

jokes.text: I was passing by when I heard this man speaking the regional dialect of my French town of Estelloude.
jokes.setup: Joey's fake French persona
jokes.punchline: His elaborate backstory about Joey speaking a regional dialect
jokes.explanation: Joey's commitment to the lie with increasingly specific details shows his desperation perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Bonjour, I am Jean-Claude Van Damme's cousin. I grew up in a small village outside Lyon where we spoke only ze French.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: My family, we were very poor. We had to share one beret between all eight children.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So when I came to America, I could only speak zis very specific regional dialect. It took me years to learn ze proper French.

jokes.text: But my French was good? / It was great. / Merci.
jokes.setup: Joey asks if his French was good despite not getting the part
jokes.punchline: The director's kind lie and Joey's response with actual French
jokes.explanation: Ironic that Joey uses actual French only after the audition is over. Shows his obliviousness.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Was my French good?

dialogue.speaker: Director
dialogue.line: Your French was great.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Merci beaucoup!

jokes.text: It's shame though, I mean, when we did it, it was pretty good. / Yeah, yeah, that's true.
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel agree not to have sex again
jokes.punchline: Their immediate reminiscing about their good sexual history
jokes.explanation: Sets up their sexual tension perfectly - they can't help but remember the good times.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So we're agreed. No more sex.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No more sex.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Remember that time in London?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, yes.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: And the time in my apartment?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: So good.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What about that—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I know. Me too.

jokes.text: Hey, do you remember that one really great time? / Oh, yeah. / You know, it was your birthday... / It was Valentine's day...
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel start reminiscing
jokes.punchline: They remember different occasions for the same encounter
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how they build different romantic narratives around the same event.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Remember when we first met?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Of course! You were so nervous, you spilled coffee all over yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That wasn't our first meeting. We met at Monica's apartment when you ran in wearing that wedding dress.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, that was the second time. The coffee incident was definitely first.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, I've known you since before you even knew my sister. You don't remember the coffee?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I remember exactly what I want to remember, Ross.

jokes.text: Even if we want it REALLY bad. / That's what WE decided.
jokes.setup: They're trying to convince themselves not to have sex
jokes.punchline: Rachel's emphasis on how much they want it
jokes.explanation: Their mutual attempt at restraint while emphasizing their desire creates good tension.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: We can't do this.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know, I know.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's a terrible idea.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: The worst.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: We would be making a huge mistake.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Absolutely.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I mean, we really, really, REALLY want to.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: ...So much.

jokes.text: I hate waste.
jokes.setup: Ross talks about their connection being a waste
jokes.punchline: Rachel's practical objection to waste
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that justify giving in to temptation with environmental consciousness.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: A waste? Ross, I'm not a natural resource!

jokes.text: Just so you know, with us, it's never off the table.
jokes.setup: Rachel previously declared sex 'off the table'
jokes.punchline: Her immediate reversal of that declaration
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that completely undermines her earlier declaration with romantic timing.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Sex is off the table.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Actually, the table is fine. The couch works too.

jokes.text: Dammit, it's never off the table.
jokes.setup: Rachel's declaration about sex always being available
jokes.punchline: Ross's frustrated acknowledgment of their pattern
jokes.explanation: Ross's frustrated acceptance of their inevitable sexual dynamic.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Sex is always available.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, that's the problem!

jokes.text: You can drink a GALLON of milk in 10 seconds? / Alright, watch me!
jokes.setup: Discussion of Joey's resume claims
jokes.punchline: Joey accepting the challenge to prove his milk drinking ability
jokes.explanation: Sets up the physical comedy ending with Joey's characteristic overconfidence in absurd abilities.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You drink milk?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, I drink milk. Why?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Can you drink a gallon in one hour?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A gallon? Yeah, sure, I can do that.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No you can't.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yes I can!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, prove it.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I will!

jokes.text: You did it!
jokes.setup: Joey attempts to drink a gallon of milk in 10 seconds
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's encouragement suggesting he actually accomplished this impossible feat
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy payoff that ends the episode on Joey achieving an impossible physical feat.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You did it! You did it! Oh my God, you did it!

tmdb_overview: Joey implores Francophile Phoebe to teach him French before an important audition, but she quickly becomes exasperated. Meanwhile, surrogate mom Erica is visiting town and tells the Bings that the baby's father may be a shovel killer. Also, Ross and Rachel grow closer after her sarcastic father, Dr. Green, suffers a heart attack.
insight: Final season Joey fumbles French, landing below Friends' 80+ standard despite brisk 3.08 jokes-per-minute pace.
